ogTitle: Cabbage Tree Island vs Cabramatta West: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cabbage Tree Island and Cabramatta West.
heading: Cabbage Tree Island vs Cabramatta West — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cabbage Tree Island, New South Wales and Cabramatta West, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cabramatta West has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Cabbage Tree Island (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cabbage Tree Island or Cabramatta West safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cabbage Tree Island has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Cabramatta West scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cabbage Tree Island scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Cabramatta West scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cabbage Tree Island or Cabramatta West?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cabramatta West has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
